title: [ExportVerilog] Add `output_file` attribute for top-level ops
body:
* Add a `OutputFileAttr` to the RTL dialect which can be used to annotate top-level operations in the SV/RTL dialect that should be emitted into a separate output file.

* Refactor the unified/split emitters in `ExportVerilog` to improve code reuse and make the two emission forms better aligned. With these changes, the only difference is that split emission implicitly pushes modules/interfaces into separate files. Unified emission also supports multiple files, which are demarcated via a banner comment in the output, mainly useful for debugging.

* Extend `ExportVerilog` to look for `output_file` attributes on top-level operations. If one is found, this overrides the assignment of an operation to a specific file. The attribute also controls whether per-file replicated ops should be included in an output file, and whether the output file should be included in the final file list.

In a future PR we might want to push the logic for unified/split Verilog emission into a separate pass that adds an `output_file` attribute to modules appropriately.

Also, we may want to add a dedicated `--output-dir` option to `firtool` in the future to control whether separate files are emitted inline (with a comment as separator, useful for debugging) or whether they should be written to disk. Right now the decision whether to emit unified or split Verilog is weirdly entangled with whether `-o` takes a file, stdout (`-`), or a directory, and whether the output files are printed inline or not. Would be great to disentangle these into separate options.
